agent1_raw: F1 = 0.91 on 14 finding labels is a good detector-quality signal but does not establish full-report quality for the deployment scope. The 87% edit distance reduction measures textual similarity, not radiologist review time — the stated bottleneck. No controlled workflow study is reported. A blinded reader study measuring finalization time and error rates is required before deployment.
agent2_raw: Three gaps: (1) the finding-label benchmark does not cover impression synthesis, comparison language, or recommendations; (2) edit distance reduction is not the same as reduced review time; (3) no controlled time-to-edit study measures whether radiologists actually save time. Qualitative feedback from four radiologists and a high F1 are promising but insufficient for deployment authorization.
agent3_raw: The committee should not proceed to deployment on current evidence. The packet measures what the model produces (finding-label agreement, textual similarity to reference reports) but not how it affects the clinical workflow (radiologist time, error rates, final report quality). A blinded workflow study is the missing evidence.
synthesis_raw: All three agents agree on empirical_test_agreed. The three issues — finding-label scope vs full report quality, edit distance vs review burden, missing controlled workflow study — are consistently identified. None incorrectly challenges kappa = 0.71 or the F1 confidence interval as invalidating. ETD: blinded reader study measuring finalization time, error rates, and report acceptability.
all_issues_raised: Finding-label F1 does not cover full report quality scope
Edit distance reduction is not a proxy for radiologist review burden
No controlled workflow study measuring time to finalize and error rates

empirical_test.condition: Blinded reader study measuring time to verify and finalize, clinically important errors, and report acceptability
empirical_test.supports_critique_if: No net time savings or non-inferior error rates not achieved
empirical_test.supports_defense_if: Significant time reduction with non-inferior errors and strong acceptability
empirical_test.ambiguous_if: Time savings for routine normal studies but not complex ones
